examples.title: Factorial using Recursion
examples.description: Program to calculate factorial of a number using recursive function
examples.explanation: The factorial function calls itself until n becomes 1 (base case). Each recursive call multiplies n with the result of factorial(n-1), building up the final result: 5*4*3*2*1 = 120
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - due to n stack frames

examples.title: Function Overloading - Area Calculator
examples.description: Demonstrates function overloading with different parameter types
examples.explanation: Three functions named 'area' are overloaded. The compiler differentiates them based on number and types of arguments passed. This demonstrates polymorphism through function overloading.

examples.title: Type Casting Demonstration
examples.description: Shows implicit and explicit type casting in C++
examples.explanation: Demonstrates all major type casting methods: implicit (automatic), C-style explicit, static_cast for numeric/class conversions, dynamic_cast for runtime-safe downcasting, and const_cast to modify const values.

examples.title: Python Equivalent - Recursive Factorial
examples.description: Python version of recursive factorial for comparison
examples.explanation: Python version shows same recursive logic. Python handles type casting implicitly - no explicit type declarations needed. Python's dynamic typing makes function overloading less relevant as functions can accept any type.

examples.title: Java Version - Function Overloading
examples.description: Java demonstrates function overloading similar to C++
examples.explanation: Java also supports function (method) overloading. Java uses 'static' for class methods that don't require objects. Type casting in Java is similar but uses different syntax: (int)doubleValue instead of static_cast<int>.
